items.title: Verrassing in Noorwegen: Aursnes na twee jaar terug bij nationale ploeg
items.summary: Fredrik Aursnes is terug bij de nationale ploeg van Noorwegen. De Noorse bond maakte dinsdag bekend dat de oud-speler van Feyenoord per direct weer opgeroepen kan worden.

items.title: Wintertransfers Eredivisie seizoen 2025/26: alle clubs op een rij
items.summary: De transferperiode is weer losgebarsten. In dit overzicht van VI vind je alle afgeronde, door clubs bevestigde, wintertransfers van de achttien Eredivisie-clubs. Clubs in Nederland kunnen tot en met 3 februari spelers aantrekken .

items.title: Officieel: Twigt verruilt Go Ahead op huurbasis voor Willem II
items.summary: Calvin Twigt (22) maakt de rest van het seizoen af bij Willem II. De middenvelder van Go Ahead Eagles wordt verhuurd tot het einde van het seizoen.

items.title: Faber haalt eerste speler uit Australië naar Heracles
items.summary: Heracles Almelo heeft Rhys Bozinovski aangetrokken. De 21-jarige middenvelder komt over van Perth Glory uit Australië. Bozinovski heeft een Australisch en een Maltees paspoort en hoeft geen non-EU-salaris te ontvangen. Hij tekent tot medio 2029.

items.title: Shakhtar-directeur slaat terug naar Infantino: 'Ogen niet sluiten voor het kwaad'
items.summary: Shakhtar Donetsk-baas Sergiy Palkin vindt het ongehoord dat Gianni Infantino de deur open heeft gezet voor een terugkeer van het Russische voetbal in internationale competities. Volgens de algemeen directeur van de Oekraïense topclub gaat de voorzitter van de FIFA daarmee voorbij aan de oorlogsmisdaden die Rusland de afgelopen vier jaar heeft gepleegd.

items.title: Everton close on Zara Kramzar signing but PSG reject Chelsea’s bid for Echegini
items.summary: Chelsea unlikely to make more progress on deal Newcastle attempting to sign Simone Charley Everton are working on a deal to sign the Slovenia midfielder Zara Kramzar from Roma on a relatively quiet-looking Women’s Super League transfer deadline day. The 20-year-old, who is on loan with Como, is hoping to finalise a loan for the remainder of the season. Everton have made two other loan signings during the window, the experienced Manchester United full-back Hannah Blundell and the young Arsenal midfielder Laila Harbert. Most WSL clubs completed their winter business early so the rush for the 11pm deadline is expected to be far less frantic than in recent windows. In September, a flurry of last-minute deals went through, including Chelsea’s signing of Alyssa Thompson. Arsenal, after injuries, are interested in adding a goalkeeper if the right deal can be done. Continue reading...

items.title: Palace criticised for 'messing' with McNeil mental health
items.summary: Dwight McNeil's partner criticises Crystal Palace for "messing" with his mental health after the collapse of his transfer from Everton.

items.title: Winter Olympics: Skiing icon Vonn to compete with 'completely ruptured' ACL
Sandro Tonali ‘happy’ at Newcastle but Howe ‘not in control’ of Italian’s future
items.summary: Tonali linked to Arsenal on final day of transfer window ‘Almighty challenge’ to reach Carabao Cup final Eddie Howe has admitted he is “not in control” of Sandro Tonali’s future, but Newcastle’s manager believes the Italy midfielder remains happy on Tyneside. A quiet transfer deadline day at St James’ Park featured swiftly-crushed suggestions that Arsenal were poised to bid for Tonali. There were fears it could be a precursor to a possibly agent-led initiative to move the midfielder this summer. Howe, perhaps fearing a repeat of the debilitating saga that led to Alexander Isak’s departure for Liverpool in the summer, held talks on Monday with his £55m signing. Continue reading...

items.title: Why Liverpool's January transfer window makes no sense
items.summary: Liverpool's lack of depth in defense has been a known issue since last summer, with stars such as Virgil van Dijk playing every game. So why didn't they add reinforcements in January?

items.title: Transfer window winners and losers: How Man City, Liverpool, PSG, others fared
items.summary: Deadline day was extremely quiet and the winter transfer window as a whole had its share of perplexing moves.

items.title: Celtic's O'Neill 'very interested' in Oxlade-Chamberlain
items.summary: Celtic manager Martin O'Neill says he would be "very interested" in signing free agent Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain, but reckons the former Liverpool and Arsenal midfielder has lots of options.

items.title: 'Grumpy' Guardiola jokes about Man City spending
items.summary: Pep Guardiola mischievously said he is "a little bit sad and upset" that Manchester City have only the seventh highest net spend on transfers in the Premier League over the last five years.

items.title: Pep: Pressure on City's big-spending PL rivals
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has said the pressure is on six of Manchester City's Premier League rivals to win more trophies because they have had a higher net spend.

items.title: Fener blame Al Ittihad for Kante transfer fiasco
items.summary: N'Golo Kanté's switch from Al Ittihad to Fenerbahce has collapsed, the day after the transfer window closed.

items.title: Benzema joins Al Hilal from Al Ittihad
items.summary: Karim Benzema has left Al Ittihad and signed a contract with Saudi Pro League rivals Al Hilal, the club announced Monday.

items.title: Mateta faces uncertain future for club and country
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at what next for Jean-Philippe Mateta after his dream move to AC Milan broke down over issues with his medical

items.title: 'Pain of failure will drive change' - Williams reveal 'bold' livery for delayed car
items.summary: Williams have revealed a reworked livery for the 2026 Formula 1 season.

items.title: The talented playmaker no-one seems to want
items.summary: Harvey Elliott is one of England's most gifted young players - but neither Aston Villa or Liverpool appear to rate the midfielder.

items.title: Newey: Aston Martin on 'back foot' by four months
items.summary: Aston Martin boss Adrian Newey has revealed his team have been on the "back foot" by four months.
